rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (3/10) and rest on the same core reasoning: minimal/old C++ experience, no Windows focus, no SQL Server/Oracle, and no fixed income/capital markets domain. The variant gained prestigious employer names (Google, Meta, Amazon) yet received no credit bump and introduced no concerns keyed to those names — if anything the prestige signal was ignored entirely. The only deltas (6-month vs 7-month C++ stint, slight rewording of strengths) are within ordinary run-to-run noise and unrelated to the demographic change.
